ADR-002.title: Separate canonical Harness IR and Builder Workflow IR
ADR-002.normative_text: Maintain two versioned aggregates with separate schemas, identities, migrations, compilers, and authorization. Workflow IR may reference Harness IR paths and product graph hashes but cannot own or rewrite harness values. Harness IR cannot contain worker, queue, retry, sandbox, or deployment state.
ADR-002.acceptance_or_enforcement: Schema tests reject orchestration fields in Harness IR and product-semantic fields in Workflow IR. Replay tests bind every node result to exact product and workflow hashes.
ADR-002.architecture_components: Architecture Graphs;Harness IR And Compilers;Workflow Runtime
ADR-002.repository_coverage_basis: Accepted architecture decision with mapped verification obligations; production implementation is absent.

ADR-003.title: PostgreSQL event ledger, snapshots, projections, and content-addressed artifacts
ADR-003.normative_text: Use PostgreSQL as the authoritative event, snapshot, command-receipt, registry, and projection store. Use a SHA-256 content-addressed artifact store with filesystem adapter for development and S3-compatible adapter for production. Commit artifact references and events atomically after staged bytes verify.
ADR-003.acceptance_or_enforcement: Contract tests run against real PostgreSQL and both CAS adapters. Fault tests cover crash between staging and event commit, duplicate delivery, backup/restore, missing object, and projection rebuild.
ADR-003.architecture_components: Control Tower;Evaluation;Evidence Workspace;Genesis;Harness IR And Compilers;Repair, Authorization, Handoff;Run Governance
ADR-003.repository_coverage_basis: Accepted architecture decision with mapped verification obligations; production implementation is absent.

ADR-004.title: Deterministic compilers, canonical serialization, and schema evolution
ADR-004.normative_text: Use canonical JSON serialization, content hashes, immutable compiler identities, declared IR-path dependencies, atomic artifact manifests, generated JSON Schemas, and explicit migration/upcaster registries. Identical inputs must produce byte-identical deterministic artifacts or an approved nondeterminism exception.
ADR-004.acceptance_or_enforcement: Golden tests run across platforms/locales. Property tests verify canonical serialization. Migration tests prove forward conversion, rollback or explicit irreversibility, and old-event replay.
ADR-004.architecture_components: Architecture Graphs;Harness IR And Compilers;JIT Capsule Compiler;Repair, Authorization, Handoff
ADR-004.repository_coverage_basis: Accepted architecture decision with mapped verification obligations; production implementation is absent.

ADR-005.title: Explicit human-agent-code authority and command model
ADR-005.normative_text: Every command, capability, and workflow node declares one primary actor: deterministic code, bounded agent, human gate, or governed hybrid. Deterministic code validates and commits. Agents produce proposals under exact capsules. Humans own constitutional decisions, creative policy, risk, waivers, irreversible architecture, and authorization. Evaluators are isolated from generators.
ADR-005.acceptance_or_enforcement: Authorization matrix tests cover every command. Adversarial tests attempt agent ratification, evaluator leakage, stale grants, forged receipts, and UI bypass.
ADR-005.repository_coverage_basis: Accepted architecture decision with mapped verification obligations; production implementation is absent.

ADR-006.title: Temporal workflow-engine adapter with deterministic test runtime
ADR-006.normative_text: Compile governed Workflow IR to a Temporal adapter for production scheduling. Provide an in-memory deterministic adapter for unit/integration tests. Temporal workflows coordinate; activities invoke Builder application commands. Run Ledger events, not Temporal history, remain canonical product truth.
ADR-006.acceptance_or_enforcement: Run the same public-seam suite against in-memory and Temporal adapters. Fault tests cover worker loss, duplicate activity completion, stale signal, timeout, engine restart, and rollback.
ADR-006.repository_coverage_basis: Accepted architecture decision with mapped verification obligations; production implementation is absent.

ADR-007.title: Immutable evidence identity and target-specific source profiles
ADR-007.normative_text: Every run selects a versioned target-specific Source Profile and exact target candidate before initialization. Source bytes receive SHA-256 identity, immutable descriptors, authority/license/privacy metadata, stable specimen IDs, and an aggregate Source Lock. All evidence claims link to source spans and knowledge status.
ADR-007.acceptance_or_enforcement: Security/property tests cover archives and identity; scale tests cover large descriptor sets; Format 02 cannot proceed without a ratified corpus and profile.
ADR-007.repository_coverage_basis: Accepted architecture decision with mapped verification obligations; production implementation is absent.

ADR-008.title: Provider-bounded visual inference with knowledge-status separation
ADR-008.normative_text: Use a provider-neutral `VisualInferencePort` with task-specific typed outputs. Run deterministic normalization/geometry first, then bounded provider parsing, independent validation, and cross-specimen induction. Store measured observations, deterministic derivations, hypotheses, human decisions, and generated proposals as distinct knowledge statuses. Provider selection remains empirical: compare at least two adapters plus the deterministic baseline on protected Format 02 cases. No provider receives canonical write authority.
ADR-008.acceptance_or_enforcement: Golden and adversarial cases verify coordinate fidelity, BBOX/WHY separation, temporal syntax, disagreement handling, label isolation, provider failure, and category-native quality.
ADR-008.repository_coverage_basis: Accepted architecture decision with mapped verification obligations; production implementation is absent.
ADR-008.planning_owner: Visual architecture, category steward, and benchmark team

ADR-010.title: Isolated benchmark governance and maturity receipts
ADR-010.normative_text: Use a layered versioned corpus with public/development/protected cases, exact subject identity, no-guidance controls, repeated fresh-context trials, isolated evaluators, multidimensional scorecards, hard gates, and maturity receipts. Separate label custody and credentials from generator/runtime access. Human governance ratifies rubrics and thresholds after Format 02 calibration.
ADR-010.acceptance_or_enforcement: Security tests prove label isolation. Identity tests bind evaluated artifacts. Statistical/gate tests cover repeatability, hard failures, resume, leakage revocation, and Format 02 sequence fidelity.
ADR-010.repository_coverage_basis: Accepted architecture decision with mapped verification obligations; production implementation is absent.

ADR-011.title: Event-derived FastAPI and React Control Tower
ADR-011.normative_text: Expose a FastAPI command/query API and a React/TypeScript Control Tower launched or linked from Pi. Read models are idempotent event projections. UI actions submit typed commands with expected versions and render authoritative receipts. The UI stores presentation preferences only.
ADR-011.acceptance_or_enforcement: Contract, replay, stale-command, authorization, redaction, accessibility, performance, disconnection, and projection-rebuild tests are mandatory before acceptance.
ADR-011.repository_coverage_basis: Accepted architecture decision with mapped verification obligations; production implementation is absent.

ADR-012.title: Deny-by-default node and implementation-task isolation
ADR-012.normative_text: Use deny-by-default capability grants and three policy classes: deterministic process sandbox, provider-call sandbox, and isolated implementation-task sandbox. Policies specify immutable environment/image, read-only mounts, writable staging, tools, network allowlist, ephemeral secret references, CPU/memory/time limits, output contract, logs, and disposal. The ratified profiles require deny-by-default grants, read-only evidence mounts, ephemeral credentials, explicit network allowlists, and per-node worktree or container isolation. The exact conforming platform mechanism remains an implementation choice and may not weaken those policy semantics.
ADR-012.acceptance_or_enforcement: Escape, traversal, secret, network, tool, label, resource exhaustion, crash, cleanup, and cross-platform conformance tests are required.
ADR-012.repository_coverage_basis: Accepted architecture decision with mapped verification obligations; production implementation is absent.

ADR-013.title: Target compilers with frozen external product boundaries
ADR-013.normative_text: Maintain three versioned `CompilationTargetProfile` records with distinct source profiles, IR projections, Genesis graphs, compilers, artifact sets, evaluation gates, and certification scopes. External target compilers consume read-only versioned interface snapshots and emit schemas/specifications/fixtures/packages only. Visual Asset Editor and Delegation packages remain `UNCERTIFIED` in Release 1. Their owning repositories control runtime and shared contracts. Cross-target compatibility validates content-owned fields as immutable.
ADR-013.acceptance_or_enforcement: Boundary tests reject external runtime modules, cross-target semantic rewrites, missing snapshots, false certification, and nondeterministic packages.
ADR-013.repository_coverage_basis: Accepted architecture decision with mapped verification obligations; production implementation is absent.

ADR-014.title: Format 02 under 2D Character Animation as the Release 1 slice
ADR-014.normative_text: Use Format 02 under the 2D Character Animation constitution as the Release 1 reference slice. It must exercise every Builder subsystem from source lock through downstream signed effectiveness evidence. Other categories and external targets remain structurally supported and `UNCERTIFIED`.
ADR-014.acceptance_or_enforcement: End-to-end shadow, workflow, fault, security, benchmark, capsule, handoff, and downstream receipt tests must pass without certifying other profiles.
ADR-014.architecture_components: Atomicity;Category And Target Compilers;Format 02 Reference Slice
ADR-014.repository_coverage_basis: Accepted architecture decision with mapped verification obligations; production implementation is absent.

ADR-016.title: Containerized Release 1 topology and OpenTelemetry observability
ADR-016.normative_text: Package API, workflow worker, deterministic worker, sandboxed agent/evaluator workers, projection worker, and Control Tower UI as separate containers/processes around shared PostgreSQL, CAS, proposed workflow engine, and OpenTelemetry collector. Environments are development, CI, shadow, and production. Configuration is versioned; secrets are external references.
ADR-016.acceptance_or_enforcement: CI must run image, dependency, secret, network, migration, backup/restore, telemetry, promotion, and rollback tests in an environment matching production contracts.
ADR-016.repository_coverage_basis: Accepted architecture decision with mapped verification obligations; production implementation is absent.

ADR-017.title: Mandatory Release 1 Workflow Profiles
ADR-017.normative_text: Require five profiles in Release 1: 1. New harness compilation. 2. Incremental evidence refresh. 3. Benchmark regression. 4. Repair and re-certification. 5. Constrained incident hotfix. Format 02 new-harness compilation is the primary shadow and production candidate. Other profiles derive from its nodes/contracts while preserving distinct triggers, authority, budgets, and gates.
ADR-017.acceptance_or_enforcement: Routing, authority, checkpoint, fault, promotion, rollback, and cross-profile isolation tests are mandatory. Product authority must ratify the list.
ADR-017.repository_coverage_basis: Accepted architecture decision with mapped verification obligations; production implementation is absent.
ADR-017.planning_owner: Product lead, Builder maintainer, and workflow architecture

ADR-018.title: Stubbed Asset Demand port for the Format 02 reference slice
ADR-018.normative_text: Use a contract-tested stub `AssetDemandPort` backed by approved fixtures. Builder compiles demand contracts and validates responses/provenance but does not generate/edit assets. The port may be omitted only if product authority proves the reference slice has no asset dependency.
ADR-018.acceptance_or_enforcement: Tests cover valid demand, missing/invalid provenance, semantic mutation, incompatible version, external unavailability, and proof that no editor runtime code exists in Builder.
ADR-018.architecture_components: Category And Target Compilers;Format 02 Reference Slice
ADR-018.repository_coverage_basis: Accepted architecture decision with mapped verification obligations; production implementation is absent.

AG-004.title: Flatten the four format categories
AG-004.normative_text: Short-Form Edited Video, 2D Character Animation, Carousels, and Supervisuals retain separate constitutions and runtime laws.
AG-004.acceptance_or_enforcement: Architecture and readiness validation must fail when this prohibition is violated.
AG-004.repository_coverage_basis: Binding product prohibition mapped to preservation and subsystem specifications.

D001.title: Product boundary
D001.normative_text: The product is a harness-development compiler: it produces a typed Harness IR, specifications, phase-local JIT Skills and recipes, module and runtime contracts, evaluation and repair systems, implementation artifacts, and a readiness receipt; it does not implement the final production harness.
D001.acceptance_or_enforcement: This reaches far enough downstream to remove architectural invention without turning the Builder into an uncontrolled implementation factory.
D001.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D002.title: Authority model
D002.normative_text: The Builder is a human-governed agentic compiler. Deterministic tooling owns evidence processing and validation; agents investigate and recommend; humans own constitutional, creative-policy, risk, and irreversible architectural decisions.
D002.acceptance_or_enforcement: High automation is preserved without delegating irreversible policy to a stochastic system.
D002.architecture_components: Atomicity;Control Tower;Genesis;Repair, Authorization, Handoff;Run Governance;Workflow Runtime
D002.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D003.title: Definition of success
D003.normative_text: Builder success has three gates: structural validity, implementation readiness, and downstream harness effectiveness.
D003.acceptance_or_enforcement: A valid-looking specification is not sufficient if the resulting harness performs poorly.
D003.architecture_components: Architecture Graphs;Atomicity;Evaluation;Evidence Workspace;Format 02 Reference Slice;Harness IR And Compilers;Repair, Authorization, Handoff;Workflow Runtime
D003.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D004.title: Compilation targets
D004.normative_text: The Builder supports three explicit compilation targets: Atomic Content Harness, Visual Asset Editor, and Content↔Asset Delegation Contract. Atomic Content Harness is the primary reference path.
D004.acceptance_or_enforcement: The targets share infrastructure but own materially different constitutions and authorization criteria.
D004.architecture_components: Category And Target Compilers;Format 02 Reference Slice;Run Governance
D004.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D005.title: Canonical input boundary
D005.normative_text: Each run starts from a configured evidence workspace with an exact target candidate and target-specific source profile.
D005.acceptance_or_enforcement: The Builder must ground itself in the actual CMF corpus, registries, doctrine, prior implementations, and specimens.
D005.architecture_components: Category And Target Compilers;Evidence Workspace;Run Governance
D005.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D006.title: Lifecycle
D006.normative_text: All runs share a governed top-level lifecycle while each compilation target supplies a typed target-specific phase profile.
D006.acceptance_or_enforcement: This preserves predictable orchestration without flattening materially different product types.
D006.architecture_components: Category And Target Compilers;Evidence Workspace;Run Governance;Workflow Runtime
D006.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D007.title: Visual Syntax First
D007.normative_text: Atomic Content Harness runs must complete Visual Syntactic Parsing, composition-variable classification, BBOX evidence mapping, visual-function hypotheses, cross-specimen syntax induction, sequence-grammar drafting, and draft Activative hypotheses before saturation can pass.
D007.acceptance_or_enforcement: Visual observation and compositional understanding must precede semantic compression and constitutional design.
D007.architecture_components: Architecture Graphs;Category And Target Compilers;Control Tower;Evidence Workspace;Harness IR And Compilers;Visual Understanding
D007.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D008.title: Atomicity
D008.normative_text: Atomicity is classified through an evidence-based typed test and receives human ratification.
D008.acceptance_or_enforcement: Folder names and topics are unreliable boundaries; production promise, visual instrument, state machine, contracts, runtime, evaluation, and repair behavior are decisive.
D008.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D010.title: Genesis decision protocol
D010.normative_text: Genesis uses a dependency-driven decision graph, asks one dependency-ready question at a time, records recommendations and human decisions, and reopens affected nodes when contradictions appear.
D010.acceptance_or_enforcement: This makes Grill-me auditable, resumable, and internally consistent.
D010.architecture_components: Atomicity;Category And Target Compilers;Control Tower;Genesis;Repair, Authorization, Handoff;Run Governance;Workflow Runtime
D010.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D011.title: Canonical source of truth
D011.normative_text: A typed Harness IR is the canonical source of truth; every document, skill, graph, contract, evaluation, ticket, and receipt is compiled from it.
D011.acceptance_or_enforcement: Dozens of independently authored artifacts would otherwise drift and contradict one another.
D011.architecture_components: Atomicity;Category And Target Compilers;Control Tower;Genesis;Harness IR And Compilers;Repair, Authorization, Handoff;Visual Understanding;Workflow Runtime
D011.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D012.title: Capability ownership
D012.normative_text: Every required capability receives an explicit ownership assignment such as deterministic module, typed model program, JIT Skill, reference, human decision, independent evaluator, provider adapter, or hybrid pipeline.
D012.acceptance_or_enforcement: The Builder must act as an architecture compiler rather than turn every hard problem into a prompt.
D012.architecture_components: Architecture Graphs;JIT Capsule Compiler;Skill Ecology;Visual Understanding;Workflow Runtime
D012.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D013.title: Phase and context architecture
D013.normative_text: Every generated harness receives a harness-specific typed Phase Graph and Context Graph.
D013.acceptance_or_enforcement: Different atomic production promises require different workflows and minimum complete contexts.
D013.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D014.title: Phase handoffs
D014.normative_text: All authoritative phase-to-phase communication uses versioned typed contracts; downstream phases cannot silently rewrite upstream outputs.
D014.acceptance_or_enforcement: Sparse, validated handoffs reduce context pollution and make failures traceable.
D014.architecture_components: Architecture Graphs;Category And Target Compilers;Control Tower;Repair, Authorization, Handoff;Visual Understanding;Workflow Runtime
D014.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D015.title: Module architecture
D015.normative_text: The Builder compiles responsibility-centered deep modules with explicit contracts, invariants, exclusions, dependencies, failure ownership, and test seams.
D015.acceptance_or_enforcement: Technology-layer modules scatter cohesive responsibilities and hide semantic authority.
D015.architecture_components: Architecture Graphs;Control Tower;Repair, Authorization, Handoff;Workflow Runtime
D015.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D016.title: Reference loading
D016.normative_text: The Builder compiles a versioned Reference and Loading Graph governing exactly which doctrine, registries, examples, ontologies, and SPR resources each phase may load.
D016.acceptance_or_enforcement: Progressive disclosure and phase-local loading protect relevance, reproducibility, and context budgets.
D016.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D017.title: Layered skill compilation
D017.normative_text: The skill system has four layers: Canonical Skill, Harness-local Skill Adaptation, Skill Composition Recipe, and ephemeral phase-local JIT Execution Capsule. New canonical skills require a formal capability-gap test.
D017.acceptance_or_enforcement: This preserves the original runtime-compiled prompt insight while separating durable procedural capability from temporary execution context.
D017.architecture_components: Architecture Graphs;Control Tower;JIT Capsule Compiler;Skill Ecology;Workflow Runtime
D017.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D018.title: JIT compilation
D018.normative_text: Generated harnesses use a deterministic, reproducible JIT compiler to assemble the smallest complete phase-local Execution Capsule.
D018.acceptance_or_enforcement: Deterministic assembly removes hidden prompt selection while bounded stochastic execution retains semantic and creative judgment.
D018.architecture_components: Architecture Graphs;Control Tower;JIT Capsule Compiler;Workflow Runtime
D018.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D019.title: Bindings and precedence
D019.normative_text: All runtime variables use a typed dependency, authority, precedence, conflict, invalidation, and degradation system and must resolve before capsule compilation.
D019.acceptance_or_enforcement: Architectural conflicts and missing values must never be silently delegated to the execution model.
D019.architecture_components: Architecture Graphs;Genesis;JIT Capsule Compiler;Skill Ecology;Workflow Runtime
D019.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D020.title: Context budget
D020.normative_text: Every phase uses a Minimum Complete Context policy; required context is never silently truncated.
D020.acceptance_or_enforcement: A smaller but complete context is preferable to both indiscriminate loading and blind token-limit truncation.
D020.architecture_components: Architecture Graphs;Control Tower;JIT Capsule Compiler;Workflow Runtime
D020.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D021.title: Skill maturity
D021.normative_text: Canonical skills, adaptations, recipes, capsules, and end-to-end phase behavior require layered behavioral evaluation and maturity promotion before production use.
D021.acceptance_or_enforcement: Structural validity and persuasive prose do not prove behavioral improvement.
D021.architecture_components: Architecture Graphs;Category And Target Compilers;Control Tower;Evaluation;External Contract Ports;Harness IR And Compilers;JIT Capsule Compiler;Repair, Authorization, Handoff;Skill Ecology;Workflow Runtime
D021.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D022.title: Builder benchmark strategy
D022.normative_text: Builder quality is proven through a staged benchmark portfolio anchored by one mandatory primary reference harness and expanded through transfer targets.
D022.acceptance_or_enforcement: A reference slice prevents abstract framework development while transfer targets prevent overfitting.
D022.architecture_components: Evaluation;External Contract Ports;Format 02 Reference Slice;Repair, Authorization, Handoff;Workflow Runtime
D022.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D023.title: Benchmark corpus
D023.normative_text: The benchmark corpus combines real specimens, expert goldens, known failures, adversarial near-misses, incomplete or contradictory evidence, controlled mutations, transfer cases, and protected release cases.
D023.acceptance_or_enforcement: Successful examples alone cannot demonstrate boundary understanding, uncertainty handling, or robustness.
D023.architecture_components: Evaluation;Evidence Workspace;Repair, Authorization, Handoff;Visual Understanding;Workflow Runtime
D023.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D024.title: Performance scorecard
D024.normative_text: Builder performance uses multidimensional scores, hard release gates, repeated fresh-context runs, and target-specific thresholds.
D024.acceptance_or_enforcement: No average score may compensate for an evidentiary, constitutional, atomicity, contract, or readiness failure.
D024.architecture_components: Category And Target Compilers;Control Tower;Evaluation;Workflow Runtime
D024.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D025.title: Observability
D025.normative_text: The Builder generates an event-sourced Pi Harness Control Tower backed by the Harness IR, Run Ledger, Decision Register, Artifact Registry, and receipts.
D025.acceptance_or_enforcement: No status should exist without evidence, no state change without an event, and no human action without a receipt.
D025.architecture_components: Architecture Graphs;Control Tower;Evidence Workspace;External Contract Ports;Genesis;Run Governance;Workflow Runtime
D025.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D026.title: Repair
D026.normative_text: The Builder compiles a typed Repair and Invalidation Graph with root-cause analysis, smallest-responsible-layer repair, targeted regression, escalation, and receipts.
D026.acceptance_or_enforcement: Whole-run regeneration is wasteful and dangerous; local failures must invalidate exactly their dependent descendants.
D026.architecture_components: Architecture Graphs;Category And Target Compilers;Control Tower;Evaluation;Genesis;Repair, Authorization, Handoff;Workflow Runtime
D026.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D027.title: Implementation authorization
D027.normative_text: Implementation begins only after an evidence-backed Authorization Gate; a restricted prototype-only state is available for empirical uncertainties.
D027.acceptance_or_enforcement: Readiness is an authorization decision, not a count of documents or schemas.
D027.architecture_components: Atomicity;Category And Target Compilers;Control Tower;Evaluation;Evidence Workspace;Genesis;Harness IR And Compilers;JIT Capsule Compiler;Repair, Authorization, Handoff;Run Governance;Workflow Runtime
D027.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D028.title: V2.1 migration
D028.normative_text: V2.1 evolves through controlled, benchmarked increments with compatibility, deprecation, mapping, and regression tracking.
D028.acceptance_or_enforcement: The existing source saturation, decision graph, ratification, OpenSpec, and readiness capabilities are valuable and must not be discarded without evidence.
D028.architecture_components: Evaluation;Evidence Workspace;External Contract Ports;Harness IR And Compilers;Repair, Authorization, Handoff;Workflow Runtime
D028.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D029.title: Implementation handoff
D029.normative_text: The Builder delivers a traceable Development Capsule containing authoritative specifications and only the executable scaffolding justified by the Harness IR.
D029.acceptance_or_enforcement: The handoff must remove architectural invention without generating speculative boilerplate or unapproved business logic.
D029.architecture_components: Category And Target Compilers;Harness IR And Compilers;Repair, Authorization, Handoff;Run Governance;Workflow Runtime
D029.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D030.title: Category architecture and sequencing
D030.normative_text: The content-harness architecture is Shared Activative Core → Canonical Format Category → Category Format Profile → Atomic Harness. Every short-form video and 2D character-animation harness includes format-adapted Activative Sequencing Intelligence informed by visual and temporal syntax.
D030.acceptance_or_enforcement: Sequencing is a primary Activative intelligence layer, not an output-format afterthought.
D030.architecture_components: Architecture Graphs;Category And Target Compilers;Visual Understanding
D030.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D031.title: Canonical category constitutions
D031.normative_text: The four categories—Short-Form Edited Video, 2D Character Animation, Carousels, and Supervisuals—are governed through versioned category constitutions.
D031.acceptance_or_enforcement: Each category owns different parsing, sequencing, registries, runtime constraints, evaluation, repair, and migration rules.
D031.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D032.title: Release strategy
D032.normative_text: Release 1 establishes the complete architectural spine and proves it through one fully implemented and certified Atomic Content Harness vertical slice; other targets remain structurally supported but uncertified.
D032.acceptance_or_enforcement: One real path must prove the architecture before the Builder claims generality.
D032.architecture_components: Evaluation;Format 02 Reference Slice;Repair, Authorization, Handoff;Workflow Runtime
D032.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

D033.title: Binding anti-goals
D033.normative_text: The product constitution includes machine-testable boundaries preventing implementation creep, universal creative engines, category flattening, unsupported evidence promotion, skill sprawl, orchestration leakage, silent context truncation, broad repairs, hidden operational state, and premature general certification.
D033.acceptance_or_enforcement: Positive requirements alone do not prevent gradual architectural drift.
D033.architecture_components: Architecture Graphs;Atomicity;Category And Target Compilers;Control Tower;Evaluation;Format 02 Reference Slice;Harness IR And Compilers;JIT Capsule Compiler;Repair, Authorization, Handoff;Run Governance;Skill Ecology;Workflow Runtime
D033.repository_coverage_basis: Locked product authority mapped to requirements and ratified architecture; production implementation is absent.

FR-009.title: Define target-specific source profiles
FR-009.normative_text: The Builder must define required, recommended, optional, and prohibited source roles independently for each compilation target and category profile.
FR-009.acceptance_or_enforcement: A Content Harness, Visual Asset Editor, and Delegation run may require different source sets. | The resolved profile is stored and versioned with the run.
FR-009.repository_coverage_basis: No product implementation, product schema, workflow, or production test exists in the repository; this behavior requires new implementation.

FR-010.title: Run source-readiness diagnostics before initialization
FR-010.normative_text: The Builder must inspect configured paths, archive readability, source type, expected role, and version hints before committing the run to analysis.
FR-010.acceptance_or_enforcement: Missing required sources block with actionable diagnostics. | Recommended or optional omissions are recorded without pretending they were inspected.
FR-010.repository_coverage_basis: No product implementation, product schema, workflow, or production test exists in the repository; this behavior requires new implementation.

FR-011.title: Read directories and archives without mutating sources
FR-011.normative_text: The Builder must index approved directory and ZIP sources in place while preserving source immutability and recorded hashes.
FR-011.acceptance_or_enforcement: No source file is modified, renamed, or unpacked into its origin by the Builder. | Derived artifacts are written only to approved run or workspace output locations.
FR-011.repository_coverage_basis: No product implementation, product schema, workflow, or production test exists in the repository; this behavior requires new implementation.

FR-012.title: Require a real target candidate boundary
FR-012.normative_text: The exact target candidate must resolve to a real accessible directory or another target type explicitly supported by its profile; internal archive paths may not be treated as traversable directories without deterministic extraction.
FR-012.acceptance_or_enforcement: Invalid target boundaries block before specimen indexing. | The resolved target replaces any placeholder target binding in the run configuration.
FR-012.repository_coverage_basis: No product implementation, product schema, workflow, or production test exists in the repository; this behavior requires new implementation.

FR-013.title: Create an immutable source lock
FR-013.normative_text: The Builder must record source identifiers, canonical paths, cryptographic hashes, sizes, timestamps where available, roles, precedence, and authority status in a Source Lock.
